title: New Roth Source and Bond Balance Controls
subtitle: Message from Ron
intro: This update adds clearer Roth conversion source choices and cleaner taxable-account balance entry for U.S. Treasury and municipal bond holdings.

items.title: Choose the conversion source
items.body: Use Higher IRA, Lower IRA, either named person first, or Percent split. A 50/50 split is supported, and the other IRA can finish the target if one side runs short.

items.title: Change Roth source by year range
items.body: The Roth Conversion Source strategy lets you change which IRA supplies conversions for selected years without changing the Roth conversion target itself.

items.title: Enter tax-free bonds by balance
items.body: Opening Year and Annual Check-In now break taxable-account balances into regular taxable investments, U.S. Treasuries, CA municipal bonds, and other-state municipal bonds. Enter balances, not interest.

items.title: Easier Cash Flow audit
items.body: Cash Flow breaks Roth conversions into separate person columns, while taxable-account income is generated from the entered balance buckets and taxed according to each bucket.
